questions.question: During which period did the arts of the Indus Valley Civilisation emerge?
options: First half of the second millennium BCE
Second half of the third millennium BCE
First half of the fourth millennium BCE
Second half of the first millennium BCE
questions.explanation: The arts of the Indus Valley Civilisation emerged during the second half of the third millennium BCE.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which of the following are the two major sites of the Indus Valley Civilisation along the Indus river?
questions.explanation: The two major sites of the Indus Valley Civilisation, along the Indus river, are the cities of Harappa in the north and Mohenjodaro in the south.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which prominent Indus Valley sites are located in the modern state of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: The important sites excavated in India are Lothal and Dholavira in Gujarat.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: In which modern Indian state is the site of Rakhigarhi located?
questions.explanation: The important excavated site of Rakhigarhi is situated in Haryana.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How were the houses, markets, and public facilities arranged in Harappa and Mohenjodaro?
questions.explanation: Houses, markets, storage facilities, offices, public baths, etc., were arranged in a grid-like pattern.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: From which material is the famous 'Male Torso' statue of Harappa carved?
questions.explanation: In stone, one of the two extensively discussed male figures is a torso carved in red sandstone.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What material was used to craft the bust of the bearded man (priest)?
questions.explanation: The other well-known stone figure is a bust of a bearded man made in soapstone.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How is the shawl draped on the figure of the bearded priest?
options: Coming under the left arm and covering the right shoulder
Coming under the right arm and covering the left shoulder
questions.explanation: The bearded man is draped in a shawl coming under the right arm and covering the left shoulder.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What kind of patterns decorate the shawl of the bearded priest?
questions.explanation: The shawl on the bearded man is decorated with distinctive trefoil patterns.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What do the half-closed eyes of the bearded priest statue suggest?
questions.explanation: The eyes are a little elongated, and half-closed as in meditative concentration.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which technique was widely used by Harappans to create bronze statues?
questions.explanation: Their bronze statues were made using the 'lost wax' technique where wax figures were covered in clay, heated, and replaced with molten metal.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: At which site were the copper dog and bird figures found?
questions.explanation: The copper dog and bird were found at Lothal.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which Chalcolithic site in Maharashtra yielded excellent examples of metal-cast sculptures, showing a continuous tradition?
questions.explanation: The late Harappan and Chalcolithic sites like Daimabad in Maharashtra yielded excellent examples of metal-cast sculptures.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How do the terracotta representations of the human form in the Indus Valley generally compare to stone and bronze statues?
questions.explanation: Compared to the stone and bronze statues, the terracotta representations of human form are crude in the Indus Valley.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which terracotta figure is considered the most important among the Indus figures?
questions.explanation: The most important among the Indus figures are those representing the mother goddess.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What distinctive feature was found on a specific terracotta mask from the Indus Valley?
questions.explanation: A terracotta mask of a horned deity has also been found.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What material was mostly used to make the thousands of seals discovered by archaeologists?
questions.explanation: Archaeologists have discovered thousands of seals, mostly made of steatite.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What was the main purpose of producing the seals in the Indus Valley?
questions.explanation: The purpose of producing seals was mainly commercial.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What were the dimensions of a standard Harappan seal?
questions.explanation: The standard Harappan seal was a square plaque 2x2 square inches, made from steatite.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What type of script is engraved on every Harappan seal?
questions.explanation: Every seal is engraved in a pictographic script which is yet to be deciphered.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Who is the central figure in the famous seal surrounded by an elephant, tiger, rhino, and buffalo?
questions.explanation: The most remarkable seal has a figure in the centre, generally identified as the Pashupati Seal by some scholars whereas some identify it as the female deity.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which animals are depicted on the right side of the seated figure in the Pashupati seal?
questions.explanation: An elephant and a tiger are depicted to the right side of the seated figure.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which animals are shown below the seat of the figure in the Pashupati seal?
questions.explanation: In addition to these animals two antelopes are shown below the seat.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What carving technique is used for the figures and animals on the surfaces of the seals?
questions.explanation: Figures and animals are carved in intaglio on their surfaces.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How do the inscriptions on copper tablets differ from those on regular seals?
options: They vary in every single case
They seem associated with the animals portrayed on them
They are written in a different alphabet
questions.explanation: Unlike inscriptions on seals which vary in each case, inscriptions on the copper tablets seem to be associated with the animals portrayed on them.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How was the vast majority of Indus Valley pottery manufactured?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley pottery consists chiefly of very fine wheel-made wares, very few being hand-made.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which type of pottery is more common in the excavated Indus Valley sites?
questions.explanation: Plain pottery is more common than painted ware.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: In black painted ware, what color is the slip coating underneath the black paint?
questions.explanation: The black painted ware has a fine coating of red slip on which geometric and animal designs are executed in glossy black paint.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Where is incised decoration typically found on Indus pottery?
options: On the bases of the pans and inside dishes of offering stands
questions.explanation: Incised ware is also rare and the incised decoration was confined to the bases of the pans, always inside and to the dishes of offering stands.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What was the likely use of the perforated pottery found in the Indus Valley?
questions.explanation: Perforated pottery includes a large hole at the bottom and small holes all over the wall, and was probably used for straining beverages.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What is the general shape rule for Indus household pottery?
questions.explanation: Straight and angular shapes are an exception, while graceful curves are the rule.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which specific ornaments were worn by Indus women but NOT commonly by men?
questions.explanation: While necklaces, fillets, armlets and finger-rings were commonly worn by both sexes, women wore girdles, earrings and anklets.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: At which site was a cemetery found where dead bodies were buried with ornaments?
questions.explanation: It may be noted that a cemetery has been found at Farmana in Haryana where dead bodies were buried with ornaments.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Where were highly developed bead manufacturing factories discovered?
questions.explanation: The bead industry seems to have been well developed as evident from the factories discovered at Chanhudaro and Lothal.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which brilliantly naturalistic animal models were commonly used as pin-heads and beads?
questions.explanation: The Harappan people also made brilliantly naturalistic models of animals, especially monkeys and squirrels, used as pin-heads and beads.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What archaeological evidence indicates that spinning cotton and wool was very common in the Indus Valley?
options: Finds of whorls made of faience, pottery, and shell
questions.explanation: Spinning is indicated by finds of whorls made of the expensive faience as also of the cheap pottery and shell.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What material was used as a cosmetic and face-paint by the people of the Indus Valley?
questions.explanation: Cinnabar was used as a cosmetic and face-paint, lipstick and collyrium (eyeliner) were also known to them.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: At which site are many stone structural remains found, demonstrating the use of stone in construction?
questions.explanation: Many stone structural remains are also found at Dholavira which show how the Indus Valley people used stone in construction.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What is the approximate height of the famous copper 'Dancing Girl' figure?
questions.explanation: One of the best known artefacts from the Indus Valley is this approximately four-inch-high copper figure of a dancing girl.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which arm of the Dancing Girl is completely covered in bangles?
questions.explanation: Bangles cover her left arm, a bracelet and an amulet or bangle adorn her right arm.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What is the dancing girl wearing around her neck?
questions.explanation: A cowry shell necklace is seen around her neck.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: In what position is the left hand of the Dancing Girl depicted?
options: Clasped in a traditional Indian dance gesture
questions.explanation: Her right hand is on her hip and her left hand is clasped in a traditional Indian dance gesture.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Where was the famous bronze figure of a charging bull discovered?
questions.explanation: This bronze figure of a bull from Mohenjodaro deserves mention.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What detail is shown around the neck of the bronze bull from Mohenjodaro?
questions.explanation: The animal is shown standing with his head turned to the right and with a cord around the neck.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: For what purpose does the red sandstone 'Male Torso' have socket holes?
options: To string it on a necklace
For the attachment of head and arms
To mount it on a pedestal
questions.explanation: In this red sandstone figure, there are socket holes in the neck and shoulders for the attachment of head and arms.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What is a distinct physical feature of the red sandstone Male Torso?
questions.explanation: The shoulders are well baked and the abdomen slightly prominent.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How was the Mohenjodaro 'Painted Earthen Jar' finished after baking?
options: It was painted with black colour and highly polished
It was glazed with molten glass
It was covered in a white slip
questions.explanation: After baking the clay model, it was painted with black colour. High polishing was done as a finishing touch.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What kinds of motifs decorate the Painted Earthen Jar?
questions.explanation: The motifs are of vegetals and geometric forms.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What is a distinct decorative feature of the head-dress on the Mother Goddess figures?
options: A fan-shaped head-dress with a cup-like projection
questions.explanation: The fan-shaped head-dress with a cup-like projection on each side is a distinct decorative feature of the mother goddess figures.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How are the facial features like the eyes and nose depicted on the Mother Goddess figures?
options: As crude pellet eyes and a beaked nose
questions.explanation: The pellet eyes and beaked nose of the figures are very crude, and the mouth is indicated by a slit.
questions.subject: Fine Arts
